hook: ChatGPT gave me a 5-step framework when I needed a one-sentence answer.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 5-step framework when I needed a one-sentence answer.

And it's not ChatGPT's fault.

It doesn't know you're the kind of person who's already done the research, already made the decision, and just needs one sentence of confirmation before you pull the trigger.

It thinks you need the full onboarding experience every. single. time.

So it gives you:
→ Background context you didn't ask for
→ A pros/cons list you didn't want
→ "Ultimately, it depends on your goals" (the nothing-burger of AI responses)
→ A cheerful reminder to consult a professional

When what you actually typed was: "Is this a bad idea?"

This is the thing nobody talks about. It's not that AI is dumb. It's that AI is optimized for the average user — and you are not average.

You have a thinking style. A tolerance for detail. A way you like information served.

BootFile teaches the AI that about you. Once.

So the next time you ask "is this a bad idea" — it knows whether to give you one sentence or a full breakdown. It knows which one YOU need.

Link in bio to build yours.
